metadata.title: Seven Fields That Make a Task Brief

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The Marcus example — three pilot sites on different tracking systems, no year range, wrong in three ways he couldn't explain — showed me the failure was a missing field, not a model error. Fields five and seven, decision rules and stop conditions, are the ones I skip. Help me: (1) take a vague brief I actually wrote and rewrite the objective as an outcome, not a process; (2) add explicit decision rules for contradictions, ambiguous numbers, and missing sections; (3) add stop conditions that make Cowork pause and report instead of guess and continue.

props.command: The Marcus example — three pilot sites on different tracking systems, no year range, wrong in three ways he couldn't explain — showed me the failure was a missing field, not a model error. Fields five and seven, decision rules and stop conditions, are the ones I skip. Help me: (1) take a vague brief I actually wrote and rewrite the objective as an outcome, not a process; (2) add explicit decision rules for contradictions, ambiguous numbers, and missing sections; (3) add stop conditions that make Cowork pause and report instead of guess and continue.
props.segment: Seven Fields That Make a Task

beats.narration_text: Every vague Cowork failure traces back to a missing field. Not a model error. Not a capability limit. A missing field. The brief is the spec for the task — and a brief with seven fields turns every crash into a fixable specification. This is Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: Write a task brief for summarizing Q3 program reports into a board document

beats.narration_text: Marcus's brief said 'summarize Q3 reports into a board document.' No year range. No specification of which financial data was in scope. No indication that the three pilot sites ran on different tracking systems. Cowork summarized what it found, presented it as complete, and the board document was wrong in three ways Marcus could not easily explain.
beats.new_visual_element: Marcus's failed brief — missing year range / financial data / pilot sites

beats.narration_text: Field one: the objective. One sentence naming the outcome, not the process. Not 'summarize the reports' — that describes the process. 'Produce a two-page board summary covering program milestones, budget status, and open risks for Q3 2024' — that names the outcome. The objective anchors scope for every field that follows.

Field five is the one most people skip: decision rules. What does Cowork do when two sources contradict each other? When a number is ambiguous? When a section is missing entirely? Without decision rules, Cowork resolves conflicts silently using its own judgment — and you find the error in the output, not in a warning.
beats.new_visual_element: Field 5: Decision rules — conflict flagged, not resolved

beats.narration_text: Field seven: stop conditions. What should Cowork do when a source is missing, a file is inaccessible, or a number does not add up? The answer is always: pause and report, not guess and continue. Stop conditions are the brief's safety valve — the instruction Cowork follows when things go wrong.
beats.new_visual_element: Field 7: Stop conditions — missing sources → pause, not guess

beats.narration_text: The test: could a stranger read this brief and know exactly what Cowork may do, what it may access, and what it must not do? Seven fields. Objective, sources, forbidden data, output format, decision rules, verification criteria, stop conditions. Missing fields guarantee bad output. Present fields guarantee a fixable spec.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — completed seven-field brief template

beats.narration_text: Build your brief before your next Cowork task. Paste this — Claude will fill the seven fields from your description.

props.command: Help me write a seven-field Cowork task brief for this task: [describe your task]. Fill in: objective, sources allowed, data forbidden, output format, decision rules for conflicts or gaps, verification criteria, and stop conditions.

Objective, sources, forbidden data, output format, decision rules, verification criteria, stop conditions. Missing fields guarantee bad output. Present fields guarantee a fixable spec. beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Seven Fields That Make a Task Brief] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
